ddls:
CREATE TABLE "data_points" (
  "object_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data point */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the state or region */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the state or region */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the data point */,
  "attribute_date" TEXT /* Date the data point was attributed to */,
  "metric" TEXT /* Metric being measured (e.g., Percent Positivity, People Tested) */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the metric */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date the data point was reported */,
  "data_type" TEXT /* Categorization of data point by type (e.g., testing, vaccination, hospitalization) */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the source_id in the data_sources table */,
  "version" INTEGER /* Version number to track changes */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the data point was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the data point was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("object_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_points_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES data_sources ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each metric */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the metric (e.g., Percent Positivity, People Tested) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the metric */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the metric (e.g., percentage, number of people) */,
  "category" TEXT /* Categorization of metric by type (e.g., testing, vaccination, hospitalization) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the data source (e.g., Clinical Laboratories, State Lab) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the data source */,
  "data_source_type" TEXT /* Categorization of data source by type (e.g., clinical laboratories, state labs, commercial labs) */,
  "url" TEXT /* URL of the data source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, policymaker, administrator) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password of the user (hashed for security) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role FOREIGN KEY ("role") REFERENCES roles ("role_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "data_point_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data point being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data point was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "action" TEXT /* Specific action taken by the user (e.g., view, download, edit) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_data_point_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_point_id") REFERENCES data_points ("object_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role (e.g., researcher, policymaker, administrator) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role with the permission */,
  "data_point_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data point with the permission */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_permissions_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_permissions_data_point_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_point_id") REFERENCES data_points ("object_id")
)
